disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetric grouped vesicles on extensor elbows/buttocks; no burrows in finger web-spaces; no household contacts itching
trigger_question_examples: Are the spots on both sides symmetrically (both elbows), and do you see thin lines/burrows between your fingers?
Is anyone else in the house itchy?
Where exactly are the spots — elbows, knees, buttocks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're on both elbows and buttocks symmetrically, I can't see any lines between my fingers, and no one else is itchy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Distribution is bilateral/symmetric, not a single unilateral dermatomal band
trigger_question_examples: Is the rash in a single stripe/band on one side of the body only?
Are both sides affected, or just one?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both sides are affected, it's not a single stripe.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Scratched areas becoming hot, spreading red, or draining pus (secondary infection)
trigger_question_examples: Are any scratched areas becoming hot, spreading red, or oozing yellow pus?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No pus or spreading redness, and no fever — just raw from scratching.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do I need to isolate from my housemates tonight?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that the symmetric extensor grouped-vesicle pattern with no burrows and no affected contacts points to a non-contagious autoimmune rash rather than scabies, so isolation is not required; arrange in-person confirmation and provide itch relief with a secondary-infection safety-net.
